channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canadians Across the Country Rally Behind Montreal Canadiens in Stanley Cup Playoffs
item.description: As the NHL playoffs continue, the Montreal Canadiens have become the focal point of Canadian hockey support, being the last team from Canada still in contention for the Stanley Cup. Fans across the co...

item.title: Malawians in South Africa Face Violence, Government Plans Evacuation
item.description: The Malawi government has reported receiving over 200 urgent calls from its citizens in South Africa, who are seeking help amid a new wave of xenophobic violence in cities such as Johannesburg, Durban...

item.title: National Dialogue Highlights Challenges and Opportunities for Nigeria’s Young Digital Creatives
item.description: For the first time in Nigeria, World Creativity and Innovation Day 2026 come with strong focus on involving young creatives in top-level policy discussion. The event bring together 68 young creatives ...

item.title: Kentucky Man Faces Trial for 2019 Murder and Kidnapping of April Arnett
item.description: Ryan 'Todd' Crawley, a resident of Kentucky, is scheduled to go to trial from May 17–28 next year for his alleged involvement in the 2019 death of April Arnett. Crawley faces charges of murder, kidnap...

item.title: Finnish AI startup QuTwo raises €25M angel round to advance hybrid AI and quantum computing platform
item.description: QuTwo, a Finnish artificial intelligence lab founded by former Silo AI CEO Peter Sarlin, has reached a valuation of approximately $380 million following a €25 million ($29 million) angel funding round...

item.title: Pakistan Climate Minister Highlights Urgency of Youth-Led Climate Action and Financing
item.description: The second edition of the Breathe Pakistan International Climate Change Conference is currently underway in Islamabad, bringing together policymakers, experts, and stakeholders to address Pakistan’s p...

item.title: Nandi Family Struggles as Son Returns from Australia Needing Rehabilitation
item.description: In Nandi, a family once celebrated the departure of their son to Australia for further studies, hoping he would bring prosperity and uplift their lives. The whole village turned out to send him off wi...

item.title: Iranian and Chinese Foreign Ministers Meet Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi met China’s top diplomat in Beijing on Wednesday, marking the first meeting between the two nations’ foreign ministers since the US-Israeli conflict with Iran tr...

item.title: Tragic baby death in Wagga Wagga underscores severe housing shortage
item.description: A heartbreaking incident in Wagga Wagga has brought attention to the severity of the housing crisis in regional New South Wales. A 37-year-old Indigenous woman gave birth to twins in a makeshift tent ...

item.title: 49-Year-Old Mother and 25-Year-Old Daughter Celebrate Joint Graduation from Same University in US
item.description: A heartwarming story has emerged from the United States where a 49-year-old mother named AP Fleetwood and her 25-year-old daughter Zharria graduated on the same day from Southern New Hampshire Univers...

item.title: Congress Extends Support to Vijay’s TVK for Tamil Nadu Government Formation
item.description: The Tamil Nadu Congress Committee (TNCC) along with the Congress Legislative Party has formally decided to support the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) in forming the next government in Tamil Nadu, as s...

item.title: Kwankwasiyya Loyalists Gradually Leave ADC After Kwankwaso Defects to NDC
item.description: Members of the Kwankwasiyya movement, wey dey loyal to former Kano State governor Rabiu Kwankwaso, don begin gradually comot from the African Democratic Congress (ADC) after Kwankwaso himself defect g...

item.title: Iran’s Blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and Its Implications for International Law and Global Trade
item.description: More than two months into the conflict in Iran, the strategic Strait of Hormuz remains a dangerous zone for international shipping, a vital route for over a third of global oil and gas trade. Followin...

item.title: Carney government focuses on trades, economy to engage young men and address labour shortages
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s government has announced plans to recruit up to 100,000 more people into the trades as part of the spring economic update. This initiative aims to support housing construc...

item.title: AIADMK and TVK Discussions Gain Traction; Vijay to Decide on Tamil Nadu Government Formation
item.description: The All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (AIADMK) has confirmed that discussions with the Vijay-led Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) are progressing positively, leaving the final decision to actor-p...

item.title: Canada to acquire new airborne early warning aircraft amid evolving northern threats
item.description: The Canadian government, under the Carney administration, plans to rapidly purchase approximately six Airborne Early Warning and Control (AEW&C) aircraft, also known as flying radars, at a cost exceed...

item.title: Philippines Hosts 48th ASEAN Summit Focused on Energy, Food, and Migrant Worker Security
item.description: The 48th ASEAN Summit is being held in Mactan, Cebu from May 6 to 8, 2026, marking the first leaders-level meeting with the Philippines as chair for the year. Over 20,000 delegates from the 11-member ...

item.title: Franco Mabanta denies role in alleged extortion attempt involving Martin Romualdez
item.description: Franco Mabanta, founder of the Peanut Gallery Media Network (PGMN), has strongly denied any involvement in an alleged robbery by extortion attempt targeting former House Speaker Martin Romualdez. Maba...

item.title: Karachi Traffic Police to Enforce Separate Lanes for Motorbikes, Buses, and Rickshaws on Sharea Faisal
item.description: KARACHI: The Karachi Traffic Police have announced a new traffic management plan requiring motorbikes, buses, rickshaws and light vehicles such as Mazdas to use designated lanes on Sharea Faisal. This...

item.title: California man sentenced to 15 years to life for murdering teenage brother with cerebral palsy
item.description: A California man, Zuberi Sharp, 26, has been sentenced to 15 years to life in prison after pleading guilty to the second-degree murder of his 15-year-old half-brother, Zayde, who had cerebral palsy an...
